checks.name: citation_density_strict_web_search
checks.detail: 3 resources declared. [1] realpython read-write-files (primary) cited at lines 35,41,56,73,83,93,97,233. [2] docs.python.org csv cited at 109,111,122,192,227,235,237. [3] realpython json cited at 126,128,147,226,236. Every primary resource cited >=1x. Only markers [1]/[2]/[3] appear -- no fabricated markers. checks.name: sequence_drift_forward_references
checks.detail: All technical vocabulary maps to this topic's concepts_introduced (text vs binary files, absolute vs relative paths, open()/modes, read/readline/readlines/write, context managers/with, csv reading, json load/dump) or prior topics (import 3.3, dict 5.2, iterables/generators 5.3, lists 4.1). Exception-handling references are deferral-only and acceptable: line 35 explicitly names 7.2/7.3 as the topics that teach error handling ('Here we focus on the I/O itself'); line 64 merely notes a missing file 'raises an error'; lines 93/97 describe that 'with' closes even 'if an error happens' without teaching mechanics. No try/except/finally, no exception hierarchy, no raise teaching. csv.writer/DictReader and json.loads/dumps are in-scope siblings of the csv/json concepts.

checks.name: length_sanity
checks.detail: Corpus body ~1955 words. Depth profile = deep, expected 1800-2200 (broader 1500-6000 acceptable). Within range.

verdict: PASS -- all four checks satisfied. Required sections present and non-trivial; strict web_search citation density met with all 3 resources cited and no fabricated markers; no forward references (exception-handling is deferral-only to 7.2/7.3, not taught); word count ~1955 within the deep profile range.
